reviews.bagelReview: They're very good, but they're too big to be eaten in one sitting, so I get them sliced and then freeze them. one half makes a good breakfast.
reviews.fillingsNotes: I don't get sandwiches here because of the price and also questionable health code history
reviews.serviceNotes: People are nice, but often forget a bagel or just forget I exist.

reviews.bagelReview: I like utopia bagels, even if they're not made "in house", they're made pretty close. They're a little oily and chewy, but in a nice way every once in a while. minus one because it's a bit on the smaller side.
reviews.fillingsNotes: Pastrami egg and cheese? how can it go wrong? lots of pastrami, lots of egg, lots of cheese.
reviews.serviceNotes: Usually a bit of a line, but adds to the experience. Choice of everything or plain.

reviews.bagelReview: I don't know where they're made, but it's a very specific, thin bagel that is pretty consistent. Better than what you can buy in a grocery store, and it makes a good sandwich, but too small for comfort.
reviews.fillingsNotes: a standard bacon egg and cheese. warm and tasty. Again, a bit small.
reviews.serviceNotes: again a bit of a line on a busy day and not much space inside. Good place to probably order before you arrive.

reviews.bagelReview: It was udis out of a bag. On a different site that might get you participation points, but not here. small, dry. small.
reviews.fillingsNotes: It's a kosher place, so no meat. I ordered sausage and it was fake sausage. that was on me for not knowing. Tasty all things considered, but not what I was hoping for. I'm fake jewish in that regard.
